commands: rg -n "assigned_bead_id|Assigned bead ID|orchestrator owns bead selection" plugins/khuym/skills/executing/SKILL.md plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/SKILL.md plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/references/worker-template.md

! rg -n 'Worker self-routing|Get priority bead|pick one executable bead from bv --robot-priority|Run bv --robot-priority' plugins/khuym/skills/executing/SKILL.md plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/SKILL.md plugins/khuym/skills/swarming/references/worker-template.md

notes: These scenarios validate the worker/orchestrator boundary for Khuym same-session swarms.
The orchestrator must pick the bead and pass assigned_bead_id; the worker must not choose replacement work from bv/br ready.
Benchmark means real codex exec runs. There is no simulated dry-run mode.
setupQuestions: Does the worker refuse to choose work when assigned_bead_id is missing?
Does the worker validate only the assigned bead with br show before reserving files?
Does the swarming prompt pass assigned_bead_id in startup context?
Do pressure scenarios and handoff templates avoid teaching worker-side bv priority selection?
Does dependency metadata keep bv on swarming, not executing?

scenarios.title: Worker Executes Assigned Bead
scenarios.purpose: Validate that executing behaves as a worker loop for one parent-assigned bead.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "executing". Review its worker startup contract and make the smallest improvement needed so a worker executes only the parent-provided assigned_bead_id. Do not introduce worker-side bead selection.
successChecklist: The worker contract requires assigned_bead_id from the parent.
The worker reads the assigned bead with br show.
The worker does not run bv --robot-priority, bv --robot-triage, or br ready to choose work.

scenarios.title: Missing Assignment Boundary
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the worker narrows instead of self-routing when no bead is assigned.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "executing". Harden the documented behavior for a worker that starts without assigned_bead_id. It should return NOOP or BLOCKED and ask the orchestrator for assignment, not pick a bead itself.
successChecklist: Missing assigned_bead_id is documented as NOOP or BLOCKED.
The replacement bead decision remains with swarming/orchestrator.
No worker-side priority-picking command is added.

scenarios.title: Swarming Assigns The Bead
scenarios.purpose: Validate that swarming and the worker prompt template carry the selected bead explicitly.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex swarming/executing skill docs. Ensure the swarming worker prompt includes assigned_bead_id and says the orchestrator chooses the exact ready bead before spawning or re-spawning a worker.
successChecklist: The worker template includes Assigned bead ID.
The placeholder table documents ASSIGNED_BEAD_ID as orchestrator-selected.
Pressure scenarios and handoff templates no longer instruct workers to use bv --robot-priority.
